notes: Model baselines. Every figure carries {value, source, url, retrieved, status}. status: verified = checked against the named public source at build time; reported = public reporting on the live 2026 crisis; estimate = derived or approximate, marked as such in the UI; brief-baseline = from the Analytica build brief pending a fresh primary-source check. Rebuild with scripts/fetch.mjs.

hormuz_throughput_mbd.source: EIA — World Oil Transit Chokepoints (H1 2025 average)
hormuz_throughput_mbd.note: Pre-crisis baseline. Roughly 20% of global petroleum liquids consumption and ~25–30% of seaborne crude.

lng_share_global.note: Essentially all Qatari (plus UAE volumes). No overland bypass exists for LNG.

global_liquids_mbd.source: EIA — Short-Term Energy Outlook, world petroleum and other liquids supply

spr_releasable_mbbl.source: IEA — emergency response; government-controlled public stocks of IEA members (~1.2 bn bbl). US SPR ~400 M bbl of that (DOE).
spr_releasable_mbbl.note: Order-of-magnitude runway figure. Actual drawdown rates are capped well below the stranded rate; the runway is a stock/flow ratio, not a release plan.

current_reported_disruption.source: 2026 crisis reporting — tanker transits through Hormuz down ~70%; 150+ ships holding outside the strait (mid-July 2026)

eps_short_run.source: Literature range for short-run oil demand elasticity; see Methodology

petroline.name: Petroline (East–West)
petroline.source: EIA WOTC; Aramco expansion reporting (7 M b/d claimed Mar 2025, sustained flows untested; ~2 M b/d in use early 2026)

adcop.name: Habshan–Fujairah (ADCOP)
adcop.source: EIA WOTC; ADNOC disclosures. Routine use limits excess capacity.

eia.label: Conservative — EIA spare bypass
eia.source: EIA WOTC — ~2.6 M b/d of unused bypass capacity. Per-pipeline allocation is Analytica's derived estimate summing to the EIA total.

expanded.label: Expanded — post-2025 Petroline expansion
expanded.source: Upper bound of public commentary (3.5–5.5 M b/d) after Aramco's claimed 7 M b/d Petroline capacity; sustained flows at that level are untested.
